assertions.description: INCORRECT: Each of the 4 input filter circuits (IN_N1..IN_N4) has a capacitor (C8, C9, C10, C11, each 1uF) with TWO resistors connecting to it: one pull-up from +3.3V (R11, R12, R13, R14) and one from the signal input (R28, R27, R22, R17). The RC detector treats each resistor-capacitor pair independently, generating 2 detections per circuit (8 total for 4 actual RC filters). All 4 output nets are unique (/7dbb71f8.../IN_N1 through IN_N4), confirming the duplication. This is a standard optocoupler output pull-up + RC debounce topology, not 8 independent filters.

INCORRECT: The third voltage divider detection (r_top=R44/43k, r_bottom=R52/10M, ratio=0.9957) is not a true voltage divider. The topology is: IC1/CMPIN (pin 8) -- R44 -- IC1/VOUT (pin 9) -- R52 -- GND, with R41 (0R) also on the VOUT net. R44 connects the INA381 overcurrent comparator input (CMPIN) to its output (VOUT) as a feedback/load network. The 'top_net' (__unnamed_24) is the CMPIN pin net, not a power supply rail. The near-unity ratio (0.9957) and the involvement of an IC output pin are strong indicators this is not a resistive divider from supply to ground.

IC1 and IC2 are INA381A3QDGSRQ1 current sense amplifiers (described as '350kHz current sense amplifier with integrated overcurrent comparator'). R35 and R36 are 22mR 4-terminal Kelvin-connection shunt resistors (Vishay WSK2512). IC1 IN+/IN- (pins 3/2) connect to R35 sense pins; IC2 IN+/IN- connect to R36 sense pins. The current_sense detection list is empty (0 items) despite this being the primary measurement function of the board. The miss likely occurs because the INA381 uses a 4-terminal shunt resistor (a Vishay WSK2512 with 4 pins), which the detector does not recognize as a current sense topology.

U5 and U6 are DRV8872DDA brushed DC H-bridge motor drivers (lib_id=Driver_Motor:DRV8872DDA, keywords='H-bridge driver motor current limit'). Each drives a motor via OUT1/OUT2 outputs (OUTA_M1, OUTB_M1, OUTA_M2, OUTB_M2 nets). The bridge_circuits detection list is empty (0 items). The bridge circuit detector does not recognize the DRV8872 as an H-bridge driver IC.

U2 and U3 are ACPL-214-500E phototransistor optocouplers that provide galvanic isolation between the main GND domain and the GNDD-EXT domain (same split as the ADuM1281 digital isolator U4). The isolation_barriers section only reports U4 (ADuM1281) as an isolation component. U2 and U3 are lib_id=Optocoupler_BJT:ACPL-214 and sit between different ground domains, but are not included in the isolation_components list.
